text: Pennsylvania should prioritize lowering taxes and reducing the cost of doing business.
The state should use more public investment to lower household costs and create jobs.
Pennsylvania should protect Medicaid and public healthcare funding, even if it requires more state spending.
Rural hospital protection should be a major state priority.
The state should significantly increase funding for public schools.
Parents should have more school-choice options outside traditional public schools.
Pennsylvania should protect access to abortion.
The state should take a stricter approach to unauthorized immigration.
Pennsylvania should accelerate clean-energy investment, even if the transition is costly.
Energy policy should prioritize lower energy prices over climate regulation.
Pennsylvania should hire more police officers and increase law-enforcement resources.
Criminal justice reform should be prioritized over tougher enforcement.
The state should strengthen worker and union protections.
Employers need more flexibility from labor regulation.
Pennsylvania should make voting more accessible, including easier mail voting.
Pennsylvania should tighten election rules even if voting becomes less convenient.
The state should intervene more aggressively to make housing affordable.
Housing affordability should be addressed mainly by reducing barriers to private development.
Pennsylvania should pass stronger gun-safety laws.
Protecting gun rights should be prioritized over new firearm restrictions.
Pennsylvania should strengthen anti-discrimination protections for LGBTQ+ people.
Religious-liberty concerns should limit some LGBTQ+ policy requirements.
Immigrants should have broader access to state and local public services.
Workforce training and alternatives to four-year degrees should be expanded.
Large employers should receive incentives if they create Pennsylvania jobs.
